title: Watch PushSecret in Keystone reconciler to cut delete lat...
description:
# GitHub Issue #273: Watch PushSecret in Keystone reconciler to remove 15s deletion-cleanup latency

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: `KeystoneReconciler.SetupWithManager` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:582-604`) currently watches `corev1.Secret`, `mariadbv1alpha1.MariaDB`, and `esov1.ClusterSecretStore` but **not** `esov1alpha1.PushSecret`. As a result, the openbao-finalizer cleanup added in CC-0079/CC-0091 only re-evaluates Pass-0 (adoption gate) and Pass-2 (garbage-collection wait) at the next periodic requeue — `RequeueSecretPolling = 15 * time.Second` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/requeue_intervals.go:22`). Each PushSecret state transition during teardown — ESO installing its cleanup finalizer, ESO releasing it after the kv-v2 purge, the API server garbage-collecting the Terminating object — therefore costs **up to 15 s** of avoidable wait, and a worst-case Keystone deletion accumulates ~30 s of pure polling latency across the three state changes the operator has to observe.

**Motivation**: Surfaced while diagnosing CI run [24852835587](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/24852835587/job/72757755094) on PR #272 (CC-0091): the keystone integration suite went from 605 s on `main` to >600 s after adding three Pass-0/Pass-1/Pass-2 wait cycles to `TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove`, `TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion`, and the new `TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption`, hitting the default `go test -timeout 10m`. The unblock fix (Makefile bump to `-timeout 20m`) treats the symptom; the root cause is that the controller is blind to PushSecret events and has to poll. A `Watches(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}, …)` with a `pushSecretToKeystoneMapper` mirroring the existing `secretToKeystoneMapper` (`keystone_controller.go:609-…`) would collapse those waits to milliseconds, both in production teardown and in envtest.

**Proposed approach**:
1. Add `Watches(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(pushSecretToKeystoneMapper(mgr.GetClient())))` to `SetupWithManager`.
2. Implement `pushSecretToKeystoneMapper` next to the existing mappers — namespace-scoped `List`, then match the PushSecret name against `openBaoBackupPushSecretNames(keystone)` to enqueue only the owning CR (avoids waking unrelated Keystones in shared namespaces).
3. Consider a `builder.WithEventFilter(predicate.GenerationChangedPredicate{})`-style filter to ignore status-only updates from ESO that do not affect finalizers/DeletionTimestamp, so steady-state ESO sync churn does not flood the queue. The two events the operator actually cares about are: (a) `pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer` added/removed, (b) `DeletionTimestamp` set/cleared.
4. Unit-test the mapper alongside `secretToKeystoneMapper` in `keystone_controller_test.go` — confirm name-match enqueue, name-mismatch skip, cross-namespace skip, list-error tolerance.
5. After landing, the integration suite's three `Eventually(…, eventuallyTimeout, …)` finalizer cycles should each shrink from ~15-20 s to ~1-2 s; the Makefile `-timeout 20m` bump can stay in place as headroom but stops being load-bearing.

**Acceptance**:
- `pushSecretToKeystoneMapper` exists with unit tests covering enqueue/skip/error paths.
- `SetupWithManager` registers the watch.
- `TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption` end-to-end runtime drops measurably (target: <30 s vs. current ~50-70 s).
- No regression in the openbao-finalizer adoption tests (`TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_*` in `reconcile_secrets_test.go`).

**Refs**: CC-0079, CC-0091, REQ-001, REQ-007. Spin-off from PR #272.

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/273

stories.title: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO installs its PushSecret cleanup finalizer (CC-0092)
stories.want: the Keystone controller to wake up immediately when ESO installs pushsecret.externalsecrets.io/finalizer on a backup PushSecret
stories.so_that: the Pass-0 adoption-wait in reconcileDeleteOpenBao clears in O(ms) instead of up to 15s of RequeueSecretPolling
criteria: After ESO installs esoPushSecretFinalizer on {ks}-fernet-keys-backup or {ks}-credential-keys-backup, KeystoneReconciler.Reconcile is invoked within one second (envtest clock).
Reconcile triggered by the PushSecret watch advances SecretsReady from Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption to Reason=OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked without any intermediate RequeueSecretPolling tick.
No change to the exactly-once FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets event contract: the event still fires at most twice across the stage 1+2+3 sequence in TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption.

Unit-level assertion: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper returns exactly one reconcile.Request with NamespacedName={Namespace: ps.Namespace, Name: keystone.Name} when ps.Name matches openBaoBackupPushSecretNames(keystone).

stories.title: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO releases the cleanup finalizer after kv-v2 purge (CC-0092)
stories.want: the Keystone controller to wake up immediately when ESO removes esoCleanupFinalizer and the API server garbage-collects the Terminating PushSecret
stories.so_that: the Pass-2 gone-wait in finalizeOpenBaoSecrets clears in O(ms), the openbao-finalizer is released, and the Keystone CR leaves etcd without accumulating 30s of pure polling latency across three PushSecret transitions
criteria: Once both backup PushSecrets return NotFound on Get, the next Reconcile is driven by the PushSecret delete event (not the 15s tick) and fires within one second in envtest.

TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption end-to-end runtime (envtest) drops by at least 10s compared to the current baseline while all three stage assertions and the ≤2 FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets bound continue to hold.

TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove and TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion both converge faster (each Eventually finalizer cycle shrinks from ~15-20s to <5s) without any assertion or timing regression.
No change in the OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked message, no change in the set of events emitted, no change in finalizer removal ordering.

stories.title: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
stories.want: steady-state PushSecret status updates from ESO (SyncedResourceVersion ticks, LastTransitionTime refreshes) to not enqueue a Keystone reconcile
stories.so_that: the workqueue stays proportional to meaningful state transitions (finalizer/DeletionTimestamp) instead of ESO's refresh cadence
criteria: A predicate on the PushSecret watch admits UpdateEvent iff finalizers set differs OR DeletionTimestamp presence differs OR GetGeneration() differs between ObjectOld and ObjectNew.

A synthetic PushSecret UpdateEvent with identical finalizers, identical zero DeletionTimestamp, and identical Generation but a bumped .status.syncedResourceVersion does NOT cause the mapper to be invoked (test asserts on an event-counting interceptor).
CreateEvent and DeleteEvent on a backup PushSecret are always admitted (first adoption observation and final GC observation must never be filtered).

A PushSecret whose name does not match any openBaoBackupPushSecretNames output is admitted by the predicate but dropped by the mapper (name-level filtering is the mapper's job; predicate operates on change semantics only).

stories.title: Control-plane developer can trace the PushSecret watch contract in the reference documentation (CC-0092)
stories.role: control plane developer onboarding to the Keystone operator
stories.want: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md to list the PushSecret watch in the Watches table and describe the pushSecretToKeystoneMapper contract
stories.so_that: a future refactor does not silently re-introduce Owns(PushSecret) or drop the predicate and regress deletion latency
criteria: The Watches table in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md gains a PushSecret row with Watch Type = Watches() and an effect that names esoPushSecretFinalizer and DeletionTimestamp as the two state transitions the operator cares about (CC-0092).
A short subsection documents the mapper: name match against openBaoBackupPushSecretNames, namespace-scoped Keystone list, list-error swallow, dedup rule.
The section cross-references CC-0079 (Pass-1 Delete), CC-0091 (Pass-0 adoption gate), and CC-0092 (this watch) so future readers see the full chain.
The RBAC table is unchanged — pushsecrets get/list/watch is already granted (CC-0079).

stories.title: Test author can verify the new mapper behaviour with the existing mapper test harness (CC-0092)
stories.want: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper to be unit-testable via the existing newMapperFakeClientBuilder / newMapperFakeClient helpers
stories.so_that: future mapper changes have a regression net aligned with how secretToKeystoneMapper is tested
criteria: Unit tests live alongside TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_* in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go.

Tests cover: name match, name mismatch, cross-namespace skip, list error swallow (via recordingListInterceptor), empty Keystone list in namespace, multiple Keystones in same namespace (only matching CR enqueued).
Tests use table-driven style where practical and follow the Gomega assertion style already used by the surrounding mapper tests.
No envtest required for any new unit test — they all run under the existing fake client harness.

requirements.description: The controller SHALL register a Watches(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(pushSecretToKeystoneMapper(mgr.GetClient()))) entry in KeystoneReconciler.SetupWithManager, replacing the existing Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) registration.
requirements.rationale: Name-based mapper enqueue is consistent with the other ESO-mediated resource watches and supports predicate filtering; keeping Owns() alongside would double-enqueue without benefit.

scenarios.name: Watch registered for PushSecret
scenarios.when: the manager is started
scenarios.then: the controller's informer cache subscribes to PushSecret events in all watched namespaces
and_then: no Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) entry remains in the builder chain
the existing Owns() entries for Deployment/Service/ConfigMap/Job/PDB/HPA/CronJob/HTTPRoute are untouched

scenarios.name: PushSecret update on backup enqueues owning Keystone
scenarios.when: a PushSecret named {ks}-fernet-keys-backup in namespace ns is updated with a new finalizer
scenarios.then: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper is invoked and returns exactly one reconcile.Request{{Namespace: ns, Name: ks}}
and_then: KeystoneReconciler.Reconcile is called once for that key
no request is enqueued for any other Keystone in ns

scenarios.name: PushSecret update on unrelated object is a no-op
scenarios.when: a PushSecret whose name matches no openBaoBackupPushSecretNames output for any Keystone in its namespace is updated
scenarios.then: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper returns an empty slice

requirements.description: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper SHALL perform a namespace-scoped Keystone List, iterate openBaoBackupPushSecretNames for each CR, and emit a reconcile.Request exactly when the event object's Name matches one of those entries.
requirements.rationale: The backup name set is bounded (2 today), deterministic, and derived from each CR's metadata.Name, so a namespace-scoped List + O(n*2) comparison is cheap and avoids a second field indexer surface.

scenarios.name: Fernet backup match
scenarios.when: obj.Name == fmt.Sprintf("%s-fernet-keys-backup", ks.Name) and obj.Namespace == ks.Namespace
scenarios.then: the mapper emits []reconcile.Request{{NamespacedName: {Namespace: ks.Namespace, Name: ks.Name}}}

scenarios.name: Credential backup match
scenarios.when: obj.Name == fmt.Sprintf("%s-credential-keys-backup", ks.Name) and obj.Namespace == ks.Namespace
scenarios.then: the mapper emits []reconcile.Request{{NamespacedName: {Namespace: ks.Namespace, Name: ks.Name}}}

scenarios.name: Cross-namespace miss
scenarios.when: obj.Name matches a backup name for a Keystone that lives in a different namespace
scenarios.then: the mapper emits an empty slice
and_then: List is called with client.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()) only — never cluster-wide

scenarios.name: Multiple Keystones, single match
scenarios.when: two Keystone CRs ks-a and ks-b live in the same namespace and obj.Name matches only ks-a's fernet-backup
scenarios.then: the mapper emits exactly one reconcile.Request pointing at ks-a

requirements.description: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper SHALL swallow and log errors returned by the namespace-scoped Keystone List so that transient cache errors do not crash the controller or lose the event.
requirements.rationale: handler.MapFunc has no error return; the pattern established by secretToKeystoneMapper / mariaDBToKeystoneMapper / clusterSecretStoreToKeystoneMapper is to log via log.FromContext and return nil on List error.

scenarios.name: List error is logged and swallowed
scenarios.when: the client.List call returns a non-nil error
scenarios.then: the mapper returns nil (no reconcile.Request)
and_then: an Error-level log entry is emitted with message "listing Keystone CRs for PushSecret watch" and the injected error attached

scenarios.name: Empty namespace is a no-op
scenarios.when: the List succeeds but returns zero Keystone CRs
scenarios.then: the mapper returns an empty slice

requirements.description: The PushSecret watch SHALL be guarded by a predicate that admits CreateEvent, DeleteEvent, and GenericEvent unconditionally, and admits UpdateEvent only when finalizers set, DeletionTimestamp presence, or Generation differ between ObjectOld and ObjectNew.
requirements.rationale: ESO writes PushSecret status (SyncedResourceVersion, conditions, LastTransitionTime) on every successful sync. Those status-only ticks must not spam the Keystone workqueue; the only deletion-path state transitions that matter are finalizer add/remove and DeletionTimestamp set.

scenarios.name: Finalizer addition admitted
scenarios.when: UpdateEvent where ObjectNew gained esoPushSecretFinalizer
scenarios.then: predicate returns true

scenarios.name: Finalizer removal admitted
scenarios.when: UpdateEvent where ObjectOld had esoCleanupFinalizer and ObjectNew does not
scenarios.then: predicate returns true

scenarios.name: DeletionTimestamp set admitted
scenarios.when: UpdateEvent where ObjectOld.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero() && !ObjectNew.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero()
scenarios.then: predicate returns true

scenarios.name: Status-only tick suppressed
scenarios.when: UpdateEvent with identical finalizers, identical zero DeletionTimestamp, and identical Generation, but a bumped status.syncedResourceVersion
scenarios.then: predicate returns false
and_then: the mapper is never invoked for this event

scenarios.name: Create and Delete always admitted
scenarios.when: the controller sees a CreateEvent or DeleteEvent for any PushSecret in a watched namespace
scenarios.then: predicate returns true
and_then: first-adoption and final-GC observations cannot be dropped

requirements.description: The change SHALL NOT alter reconcileDeleteOpenBao, finalizeOpenBaoSecrets, hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets, or the exactly-once FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets event contract established by CC-0079 / CC-0091.
requirements.rationale: The watch is a latency fix, not a behaviour change. All CC-0079 / CC-0091 integration tests must continue to pass without assertion edits.

scenarios.name: All CC-0091 unit tests green
scenarios.when: TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_* in reconcile_secrets_test.go is run
scenarios.then: every test passes without source modification

scenarios.name: Adoption race integration still correct
scenarios.when: TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption runs under envtest
scenarios.then: all three stage assertions (WaitingForESOAdoption → OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked → GC) hold

scenarios.name: No backup change in reconcile_secrets.go
scenarios.when: git diff is inspected
scenarios.then: no function body in reconcile_secrets.go is modified by this feature
and_then: openBaoBackupPushSecretNames signature and return order are untouched

requirements.description: The documentation SHALL be updated to reflect the new PushSecret watch and mapper/predicate contract.
requirements.rationale: keystone-reconciler.md is the canonical reference for the controller's watch topology; an undocumented Watches entry invites a future refactor to drop it.

scenarios.name: Watches table row present
scenarios.when: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md is rendered
scenarios.then: the Watches table contains a PushSecret row with Watch Type = Watches() and an effect paragraph referencing pushSecretToKeystoneMapper, esoPushSecretFinalizer, and DeletionTimestamp as the two state transitions of interest

scenarios.name: Mapper subsection present
scenarios.when: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md is rendered
scenarios.then: a subsection describes the mapper's name-match rule, namespace scoping, list-error swallow, and deduplication
and_then: the subsection cross-references CC-0079 (Pass-1 Delete) and CC-0091 (Pass-0 adoption gate)

scenarios.name: No RBAC drift
scenarios.when: the RBAC table is inspected
scenarios.then: pushsecrets retains get/list/watch/create/update/patch/delete (already granted for CC-0079); no new entry is needed

requirements.description: The PushSecret watch SHALL NOT fan out to unrelated Keystone CRs in shared namespaces (no-op dedup contract).
requirements.rationale: A shared tenant namespace can host multiple Keystone CRs; a PushSecret event matching one CR's backup name pattern must not wake the others.

scenarios.name: Only the matching CR is enqueued
scenarios.when: ns contains ks-a and ks-b, and obj.Name == ks-a-fernet-keys-backup
scenarios.then: the mapper returns exactly one request for ks-a

scenarios.name: Non-backup PushSecret in ns is dropped
scenarios.when: obj.Name == "some-other-pushsecret" in a namespace with one Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the mapper returns an empty slice

tasks.title: Implement pushSecretToKeystoneMapper in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go alongside the existing mappers. Namespace-scoped Keystone List; for each CR iterate openBaoBackupPushSecretNames and match obj.Name; on List error log with log.FromContext and return nil. Dedup via a map[types.NamespacedName]struct{} before building the []reconcile.Request slice. Includes unit tests TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_FernetBackupEnqueuesKeystone, _CredentialBackupEnqueuesKeystone, _UnrelatedNameReturnsNil, _MultipleKeystonesOnlyMatchingCREnqueued, _EmptyNamespaceReturnsNil added to keystone_controller_test.go using newMapperFakeClient/newMapperFakeClientBuilder (CC-0092, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-007).

Add TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_CrossNamespaceReturnsNil and TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_ListErrorIsSwallowed to keystone_controller_test.go, reusing the existing recordingListInterceptor helper to assert (a) List carries client.InNamespace(obj.Namespace), (b) a list error does not panic and returns nil. Mirror the style of TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_IndexedLookupScopedToNamespace and TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_IndexedLookupErrorLoggedAndSwallowed (CC-0092, REQ-002, REQ-003).

Define the PushSecret watch predicate as an unexported predicate.Funcs value (or a small struct returning predicate.Predicate) in keystone_controller.go named pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate. UpdateFunc returns true iff finalizers sets differ (use equality.Semantic or a sorted-slice compare), or DeletionTimestamp presence differs (IsZero mismatch), or GetGeneration() differs. CreateFunc/DeleteFunc/GenericFunc return true. Use sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/predicate (CC-0092, REQ-004).

Add predicate unit tests to keystone_controller_test.go: TestPushSecretPredicate_FinalizerAddAdmitted, _FinalizerRemoveAdmitted, _DeletionTimestampSetAdmitted, _StatusOnlyUpdateSuppressed, _CreateAndDeleteAlwaysAdmitted, _GenerationChangeAdmitted. Construct event.UpdateEvent/CreateEvent/DeleteEvent directly; no fake client needed. Assert the boolean return of pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate.Update/Create/Delete (CC-0092, REQ-004).

In KeystoneReconciler.SetupWithManager (keystone_controller.go around line 636-680), remove the Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) entry and the preceding comment. Add a Watches(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(pushSecretToKeystoneMapper(mgr.GetClient())), builder.WithPredicates(pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate)) chain link beneath the ClusterSecretStore Watches. Update the import block to include sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/builder and .../pkg/predicate (verify tidy). Document the replacement in an inline comment referencing CC-0092 (CC-0092, REQ-001, REQ-004).

Run `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, and `golangci-lint run ./operators/keystone/...` to confirm the new imports and function symbols are resolved and the watch registration compiles. Fix any lint findings (e.g. unused parameter lint on mapper signature, comment punctuation) inline (CC-0092, REQ-001).

Run the CC-0079 / CC-0091 regression suite: `go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... -run 'TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets|TestReconcileDeleteOpenBao|TestSecretToKeystoneMapper'`. Confirm all pass without source edits to reconcile_secrets.go or its tests. No task should mutate those files (CC-0092, REQ-005).

Run the envtest integration tests that exercise the adoption/deletion path: `go test -tags=integration -timeout=20m ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... -run 'TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption|TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove|TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion'`. Record stage-transition wall-clock before/after (use `-v` + the existing Eventually log lines) and confirm each Eventually cycle drops from ~15-20s to <5s. All assertions (WaitingForESOAdoption, OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked, stage1Finalizing ≤ 1, cross-stage ≤ 2) must continue to hold (CC-0092, REQ-005).

Extend the Watches table in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md (the section that today lists Secret / MariaDB / ClusterSecretStore as Watches() rows) with a new PushSecret row: Watch Type = Watches(), Effect paragraph naming pushSecretToKeystoneMapper, esoPushSecretFinalizer, and DeletionTimestamp as the state transitions of interest, citing CC-0092. Add a short subsection under the existing Secret Field Indexer block describing the mapper's name-match / namespace-scope / list-error-swallow / dedup contract and cross-referencing CC-0079 (Pass-1 Delete) and CC-0091 (Pass-0 adoption gate) (CC-0092, REQ-006).

test_specifications.story: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO installs its PushSecret cleanup finalizer (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: A PushSecret named {ks}-fernet-keys-backup in ks.Namespace enqueues exactly one reconcile.Request for ks.

test_specifications.story: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO installs its PushSecret cleanup finalizer (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: A PushSecret named {ks}-credential-keys-backup in ks.Namespace enqueues exactly one reconcile.Request for ks.

test_specifications.story: Test author can verify the new mapper behaviour with the existing mapper test harness (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: A PushSecret whose Name does not match any openBaoBackupPushSecretNames entry in its namespace returns an empty slice.

test_specifications.story: Test author can verify the new mapper behaviour with the existing mapper test harness (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: With Keystone ks in ns-a and a PushSecret named {ks}-fernet-keys-backup in ns-b, the mapper returns nil and the recorded List uses client.InNamespace("ns-b") only.

test_specifications.story: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: With ks-a and ks-b in the same namespace, a backup PushSecret for ks-a returns exactly one request for ks-a and none for ks-b.

test_specifications.story: Test author can verify the new mapper behaviour with the existing mapper test harness (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: With recordingListInterceptor injecting a List error, the mapper returns nil without panicking and a single List attempt is recorded.

test_specifications.story: Test author can verify the new mapper behaviour with the existing mapper test harness (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: With no Keystone CRs in the event's namespace, the mapper returns an empty slice (handler contract allows nil-equivalent).

test_specifications.story: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: UpdateEvent whose ObjectNew gained esoPushSecretFinalizer returns true from the predicate.

test_specifications.story: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: UpdateEvent where ObjectOld has esoCleanupFinalizer and ObjectNew does not returns true.

test_specifications.story: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: UpdateEvent with identical finalizers, identical zero DeletionTimestamp, identical Generation but a bumped status.syncedResourceVersion returns false.

test_specifications.story: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: CreateEvent and DeleteEvent on any PushSecret (including one whose name does not match a backup pattern) return true — name-level filtering is the mapper's responsibility.

test_specifications.story: Operator is not spammed by status-only ESO sync churn (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: UpdateEvent where ObjectOld.Generation != ObjectNew.Generation (spec mutation) returns true.

test_specifications.story: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO releases the cleanup finalizer after kv-v2 purge (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: Existing test continues to pass. Runtime measurement: the stage transitions (WaitingForESOAdoption → OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked → GC) each converge in <5s under envtest (was ~15-20s), while the stage1Finalizing ≤ 1 and cross-stage ≤ 2 FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets bounds still hold.

test_specifications.story: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO releases the cleanup finalizer after kv-v2 purge (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: Existing test continues to pass without any assertion change. Wall-clock converges faster because each adoption/release waits on a watch event rather than a 15s tick.

test_specifications.story: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO releases the cleanup finalizer after kv-v2 purge (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: Existing test continues to pass without any assertion change. The brownfield path (no backup PushSecrets ever created) is unaffected — no PushSecret events, mapper never fires.

test_specifications.story: Operator reconciles Keystone within watch-event latency when ESO releases the cleanup finalizer after kv-v2 purge (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: All existing TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_* tests continue to pass without modification — CC-0079/CC-0091 Pass-0/1/2 semantics are unchanged by this feature.

test_specifications.story: Control-plane developer can trace the PushSecret watch contract in the reference documentation (CC-0092)
test_specifications.expected: Reviewer confirms the Watches table contains a PushSecret row citing CC-0092 and a subsection describes the mapper's name-match / namespace-scope / list-error-swallow contract and cross-references CC-0079, CC-0091.

review_criteria: BLOCKER: KeystoneReconciler.SetupWithManager contains exactly one registration for esov1alpha1.PushSecret — a Watches(…) with handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(pushSecretToKeystoneMapper(mgr.GetClient())) and builder.WithPredicates(pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate). No Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) remains (CC-0092, REQ-001).

BLOCKER: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper List uses client.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()) only (no cluster-wide List) — verifiable via recordingListInterceptor in the cross-namespace test (CC-0092, REQ-002).
BLOCKER: pushSecretToKeystoneMapper never returns an error from handler.MapFunc; a List error is logged via log.FromContext and mapper returns nil (CC-0092, REQ-003).

BLOCKER: The predicate suppresses UpdateEvents whose finalizers, DeletionTimestamp presence, and Generation are all unchanged — including status-only ticks from ESO. Verified by TestPushSecretPredicate_StatusOnlyUpdateSuppressed (CC-0092, REQ-004).

BLOCKER: reconcile_secrets.go, reconcile_secrets_test.go, and the three TestIntegration_* tests in integration_test.go are unchanged by this feature. A `git diff --name-only` in review must NOT list reconcile_secrets.go in the changeset (CC-0092, REQ-005).
CRITICAL: All TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_* and TestPushSecretPredicate_* unit tests pass under `go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/...` with race detection enabled.

CRITICAL: All three envtest integration tests (TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption, TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove, TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion) pass with no assertion edits. Each Eventually finalizer-wait cycle converges in <5s (documented in PR description).
CRITICAL: The feature does NOT add a field indexer for PushSecret — the backup name set is bounded (2) and derivable from keystone.Name, so an in-memory compare is the right call.
MINOR: All new code carries CC-0092 in doc comments per FEATURE_ID_REQUIRED (rule 11). Package imports are ordered per gofumpt/goimports conventions already used in keystone_controller.go.

MINOR: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md Watches table has exactly one new PushSecret row (CC-0092) and a matching mapper subsection; no duplicate paragraphs, no stale references to Owns(PushSecret).
implementation_notes:
Architectural decisions:

1. Watches+mapper over Owns. CC-0091 introduced Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) as a first pass; this feature replaces it with the Watches+pushSecretToKeystoneMapper pattern used by the other ESO-mediated resources (Secret, ClusterSecretStore) in the same controller. The trade-off is a bounded namespace-scoped Keystone List per event vs. Owns()'s owner-ref extraction, but it unlocks predicate filtering and keeps the mapper/watch topology consistent across all ESO-controlled resources. The Secret reference doc already documents why Watches+mapper is preferred for ESO-controlled objects — we mirror that reasoning for PushSecret.

2. No field indexer. openBaoBackupPushSecretNames is a 2-element deterministic slice derived from keystone.Name. An indexer would require a new key, invalidation logic, and a cross-reference from every name extractor site. Lookup is O(n_keystones_in_ns * 2) string compares per PushSecret event — negligible in practice (shared namespaces rarely exceed a handful of CRs) and cheaper than the indexer registration surface. If/when the backup set grows or shared namespaces scale to hundreds of Keystones, revisit under a separate feature.

3. Predicate semantics. ESO bumps .status.syncedResourceVersion and conditions on every successful refresh (~1h interval but can be quicker under store reconfiguration). Those ticks carry no state relevant to the openbao-finalizer. The predicate filters exactly three dimensions that Pass-0/Pass-1/Pass-2 observe: finalizers set diff, DeletionTimestamp presence diff, Generation diff. Create/Delete/Generic always admitted so first-adoption and final-GC observations are never dropped.

4. Removing Owns is safe. Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) was added by CC-0091 explicitly for this latency fix. The Watches+mapper registration is a strict superset — it enqueues the same requests on the same events AND supports predicate filtering. Running both would produce redundant enqueues (coalesced by the workqueue but wasteful).

Architecture / data-flow sketch:

  +-------------+          +--------------+          +---------------------+
  |  ESO ctrl   |--update->|  PushSecret  |--event-->| controller-runtime  |
  +-------------+          +--------------+          |  informer cache     |
                                                      +----------+----------+
                                                                 | UpdateEvent
                                                                 v
                                                     +--------------------------+
                                                     | pushSecretRelevantChange |
                                                     |        Predicate         |
                                                     +-----+--------------------+
                                                           | (admit?)
                                                           v
                                              +----------------------------+
                                              | pushSecretToKeystoneMapper |
                                              |   List Keystones in ns     |
                                              |   match openBaoBackup...   |
                                              +-------------+--------------+
                                                            |
                                                            v
                                              +----------------------------+
                                              |  KeystoneReconciler.       |
                                              |  Reconcile → reconcile-    |
                                              |  DeleteOpenBao Pass-0/1/2  |
                                              +----------------------------+

Pitfalls to avoid:

- Do not touch openBaoBackupPushSecretNames — its return order is load-bearing for the existing adoption tests (fernet first, credential second).
- Do not add the predicate to the existing Secret/MariaDB/ClusterSecretStore watches — their behaviour is already stable and a predicate change would be scope creep.
- Do not move the watch registration above registerSecretNameIndex — the field indexer must be registered before any Watches that relies on MatchingFields.
- The predicate must NOT inspect resource version or conditions — a naive 'any status change' filter would inadvertently block legitimate status-only transitions that the operator needs in the future.
- builder.WithPredicates applies to the watch it is chained to, not to the whole controller — place it inside the Watches(...) call, not at the end of the builder chain.

Canonical reference files to mirror style/patterns: operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go lines 683-809 for existing mappers; keystone_controller_test.go lines 790-3100 for mapper test style, recordingListInterceptor, newMapperFakeClient; integration_test.go lines 3195-3400 for the DeleteRacingESOAdoption assertion topology.
